steps.title: Identify the subject of the relative clause
steps.content: The blank is the verb in "which ______ located in Newark, New Jersey." The relative pronoun "which" refers to the singular noun "Newark Liberty International Airport" and is the subject of this clause.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement and eliminate
steps.content:
A singular subject requires a singular verb. The airport is a permanent, present-tense fact, so a simple present singular verb is needed.

A. is — singular present; agrees with singular "which." Correct.
B. are — plural present; does not agree with singular "which." Eliminated.
C. were — plural past; wrong number and wrong tense for a permanent location. Eliminated.
D. have been — plural present perfect; wrong number. Eliminated.

A is correct.
